<!-- Topic 1: Very Brief Review of Static -->
<!-- Slides 4-7 -->

# Very Brief Review of Static
<!-- Slide 4 -->

## What Does Static Remember? {.smaller}

+ A `static` local variable is created once.
+ It keeps its value between function calls.
+ The name is still visible only inside that function.

::: notes
Slides 4-7
:::

<!-- Slide 5 -->

---

## Static Local Example {.smaller}

```{.cpp}
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

void showCount();

int main() {
    showCount();
    showCount();
    showCount();
    return 0;
}

void showCount() {
    static int count = 0;
    count++;
    cout << count << endl;
}
```

<!-- Slide 6 -->

---

## Bridge to Patterns

+ `static` is a mechanism, not the main design idea.
+ It can make one stored value available through one controlled function.
+ Singleton uses that idea to describe a larger design choice.

<!-- Slide 7 -->
